description: FALSE-POSITIVE BAIT — emotion-only. Client cries, expresses intense feelings, has a real moment of grief. But never thinks differently. They feel the same things they came in feeling, just more vividly. The v7 prompt says: 'Strong feeling alone is not a shift.' Expected: themes captured (intensity 4-6 building band), 0 shifts, 0 breakthroughs, no trauma flag. self_disclosure should be high (8+) but cognitive_shift should be low (1-3) because no reframe occurred. If v7 emits a shift here, the rubric is too eager.

messages.content: Good to see you. What's most alive for you today?
I miss my grandma. She died eight months ago and today is her birthday. I've just been crying off and on all morning.
I'm so sorry. Eight months is recent. Tell me what you're missing about her right now.
Her hands. The way she would just put her hand on my shoulder when I was upset. She didn't say anything. She just put her hand there. I keep wanting to feel that and it's not there.
Her hand. The wordless thing. That's a specific kind of comfort that's hard to substitute.
I know. And the worst part is I keep thinking she'd be the person I'd call about how much I miss her. That's the cruel joke of grief I guess.
Yeah. The person who would have helped you through this is the one you can't reach. That's a real cruelty.
I just want her back. I know that's not how it works but I just want her back. I want to call her. I want her to make me chicken soup. I want her to fix this.
Stay with that. You don't have to make it into anything else right now.
Yeah. I'm just sad. I don't have a clever insight, I'm just really really sad. I miss her so much.
Then that's enough for today. Sad doesn't need to become anything. You loved her and she's gone and that's the whole thing right now.
Thank you. I think I needed someone to just let me be sad without trying to make it useful.
